bullets: The US-Iran deal and easing Hormuz risk sent crude sharply lower, pulling PP down across the board.
Producers are clearing inventory into the weakness — Hyosung Vina and Lotte both cutting to move volume.
Plentiful Korean and Chinese cargo keeps import competition fierce; buyers stay on the sidelines.
PE keeps sliding on the crude collapse and persistently weak regional demand — no floor in sight yet.
Deep US offers are resetting expectations lower, with finalized import deals at multi-month lows.
Looming supply from the Prefchem restart adds further downward pressure into late June.
PVC is holding up better than PP/PE — drifting lower rather than falling, as feedstock pressure is milder.
Chinese coal-based cargo is undercutting oil-based supply, capping any upside in the region.
July offers are out with modest cuts, but sellers are holding firm to negotiate rather than chase volume.

editorial.changedNote: 28 of 50 fell, led by PE and PP

summary: US-Iran deal / Strait of Hormuz hopes pushed crude down; SEA PE falling on weak demand and US deep offers.
Finalized US LLDPE imports now 900-940 CIF; Petrochina LLDPE 1,020-1,030; Prefchem PE restart looms.
paras: According to the ICIS price assessment announced on the last trading day of last week, June 19, the price level of LDPE Film in Vietnam last week decreased by an additional 50 USD/ton compared to the previous week, dropping to 1300-1420 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE price also decreased by 50 USD/ton in the low-end segment but dropped slightly by 20 USD/ton in the high-end segment, down to 1020-1130 USD/ton/CFR; HDPE Film price decreased by 50 USD/ton down to 1100-1150 USD/ton/CFR; HDPE injection and blow both decreased by 50 USD/ton in both segments, dropping to 1150-1250 USD/ton/CFR.

Broadening to the region, in the past week, the US-Iran cooperation memorandum sparked hopes of reopening the Strait of Hormuz and increasing supply from the Middle East, causing buyers to stay on the sidelines. In this price-decreasing environment, discussions were limited to a low level throughout the week. China's PE export market remained stagnant, with sparse transactions. In the Southeast Asian PE import market, prices fell continuously, pressured by weak demand and increasingly fierce competition. US-origin PE offered deeply to Asia further heightened the market's cautious sentiment.
Returning to the Vietnamese market today, June 22, quick surveys show that the downward trend of PE has not yet shown signs of stopping, and many items continue to decrease rapidly.

In the North, Chinese-origin LDPE Film is commonly offered around 43,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; Middle Eastern-origin LDPE Film such as Sabic, Tasnee, Borouge is commonly offered in the range of 44,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; Lotte Titan Malaysia's LDPE Film 260GG is offered for retail at 34,500 VND/kg/ with VAT... dropping about 1,000 VND/kg compared to late last week.

With LLDPE, today's offer prices for LLDPE Film are commonly recorded in the range of 33,000-34,500 VND/kg/ with VAT. Notably, a major distributor offered Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ at 33,500 VND/kg/ with VAT at Hai Phong port; some commercial sellers offered at 34,500 VND/kg/ with VAT for retail buyers around the Hanoi area. In the low-price segment, Chinese LLDPE Film 7042/7042N is offered at 33,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, but recorded transaction prices are only around 32,000 VND/kg/ with VAT... This new price level decreased by 500-1,000 VND/kg compared to last week, depending on the grade and supplier.

HDPE is the item that has held prices more firmly than LDPE and LLDPE over the past few weeks. Today, a relatively large distributor in the North offered Qamar's HDPE Film F1 at 36,000 VND/kg/ excluding VAT, and 38,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. Immediate delivery inventories of this item among commercial sellers are not large. Other immediate-delivery grades are also low in inventory and maintain high prices, such as Thailand PTT's HDPE yarn 5000S, recorded with a supplier offering at 39,000 VND/kg/ with VAT in the South and 39,300 VND/kg/ with VAT in the Central region...

Regarding imports, late last week, a distributor offered US Cynpol's LDPE Film 0423F (MI 4) to Vietnam at 1220 USD/ton/CIF; offered Cynpol's HDPE injection at 1000 USD/ton/CIF. This distributor also offered US-origin LLDPE Film (MI 1, MI 2 grades without additives) from Cynpol at 1000 USD/ton/CIF, and US-origin LLDPE Film 118A (MI 1, without additives) at a very cheap price of 960 USD/ton/CIF... but these are distant-delivery goods, arriving in the country around October 2026.

However today, there is information that some buyers received finalized import prices for US-origin LLDPE Film at 900-940 USD/ton/CIF depending on the supplier (grade codes are not yet clear); or US-origin HDPE blow at 970-980 USD/ton/CIF. Late last week, the finalized selling price for Chinese Petrochina's LLDPE Film to Vietnam was recorded at 1020-1030 USD/ton/CIF...
In addition, Malaysia's Prefchem PE complex is expected to restart operations late this June, which might further increase supply pressure...

summary: ICIS (Jun 19): PP yarn/inj -80/-50 to 1,040-1,130 CFR; regional copo -60, BOPP -10/-40 on crude slump.
Hyosung Vina cut PP yarn another 500 to 34,700 (still too high to sell); copo to ~37,000 VND/kg.
Lotte closed PP yarn H5300 at 1,030-1,040 CIF; NSRP 114G deals from 32,500; Korean homo to 34,000.
paras: According to the ICIS price assessment announced on the last trading day of last week, June 19, the price level of PP yarn/injection in Vietnam last week decreased sharply by 80 USD/ton in the low-end segment and by 50 USD/ton in the high-end segment, dropping to 1040-1130 USD/ton/CFR. Broadening to the Southeast Asia and China region, prices of various PP types last week also decreased simultaneously, with BOPP down 10-40 USD/ton, PP yarn/injection/film down 20-70 USD/ton, and PP block copo and random copo down 60 USD/ton compared to the week before.

PP prices decreased sharply due to the impact of falling crude oil prices, following a temporary agreement signed between the US and Iran and expectations of smooth trade flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Most buyers have postponed purchases in hopes of getting more competitive prices, leading to weak demand. Some regional suppliers decreased offer prices to quickly clear out inventory, even resulting in localized panic selling in a few regional markets. However, completed transactions remain limited.

Returning to the Vietnamese market, through a quick survey today, June 22, the Hyosung Vina plant decreased its PP yarn offer price by an additional 500 VND/kg compared to late last week, dropping to 34,700 VND/kg/ with VAT in the South. Even so, this price level for PP homo is still considered too high and difficult to achieve domestic sales. Hyosung's PP copo also decreased to around 37,000 VND/kg/ with VAT.

Customs data shows that Hyosung Vina is still actively pushing PP exports. In the past May, Hyosung Vina ranked first among PP exporters with the largest volume from Vietnam, with total exports reaching nearly 62,000 tons. Nghi Son Refinery and Petrochemical (NSRP) ranked second in export volume with over 24,200 tons, and LSP ranked third with over 23,000 tons...
With high export volumes, combined with domestic offer volumes recently, it shows that PP plants of Hyosung Vina, NSRP, and BSR are still operating at high capacity with large output.

In the North today, many commercial companies offered NSRP's PP 114G at 32,800-33,300 VND/kg/ with VAT, with finalized transaction prices from 32,500 VND/kg/ with VAT – moving sideways compared to the lowest finalized selling price late last week. Even so, buyers still sign imports very hesitantly, perceiving that the finalized transaction price of this week could decrease further. In fact, also in the North, Chinese-origin PP yarn was recorded with finalized prices around 32,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, while the popular offer price from distributors is around 32,500 VND/kg/ with VAT...

Some South Korean-origin PP items witnessed a very sharp decrease, in which PP homo was recorded at 34,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, and PP copo at 36,500-37,000 VND/kg/ with VAT... Recently, Lotte's South Korean-origin PP goods offered to Vietnam have been abundant and prices continue to decrease, creating competitive pressure against Chinese-origin goods. Late last week, Lotte finalized a large-volume sale of PP yarn H5300 at only 1030-1040 USD/ton/CIF.

summary: Erdos oil-based SG-5 21,300 / Yihua 21,200 incl VAT, down 300-400 on the week; H66/266RT 22,000.
TPC Vina July SG660/SP660 20,200-20,400 ex-VAT; AGC Vina PM66R 19,700-20,000; both holding to negotiate.
China operating rate ~69.47%; coal-based SG-5 offered to India 670 CIF vs oil-based 740+.
paras: Today, June 22, a major distributor is offering oil-based PVC SG-5 of Erdos (China) origin at VND 21,300/kg including VAT at Hai Phong port; another distributor is offering PVC SG-5 of Yihua (China) origin at VND 21,200/kg including VAT at Yen Vien station (Hanoi)... These offer prices are down VND 300–400/kg from the previous week.

As for oil-based PVC, also in the north, a distributor is offering PVC H66 from CGPC (Taiwan) at VND 22,000/kg including VAT; offers of PVC 266RT from AVT (Vinythai plant, Thailand, part of AGC Group) are also at VND 22,000/kg including VAT – down VND 200/kg from the lowest price the previous week...

Vietnam's two PVC producers, AGC Vina and TPC Vina, both posted selling-price offers last week for PVC shipments delivered in July 2026. TPC's offer for PVC SG660/SP660 was VND 20,200–20,400/kg excluding VAT, depending on volume. Including the full 8% VAT, the offer ranges from VND 21,600–22,032/kg including VAT. AGC Vina (ACVN) offered PVC PM66R at VND 19,700–20,000/kg excluding VAT, depending on volume. At the start of this week, both plants are holding prices to focus on negotiations and closing orders.
This development shows that PVC prices this week are moving sideways to slightly lower, with high competitiveness.

Looking more broadly at the region, since mid-June China's overall PVC operating rate has been recorded at about 69.47%, up 1.68 percentage points from the start of the month.  Chinese suppliers are offering coal-based PVC SG-5 to Indian customers at USD 670/ton CIF, for forward delivery in September 2026. This offer price is quite competitive, as oil-based product offered into India is running at USD 740/ton CIF and above.
